_meta: Secret commands. Checked before the parser. Most fire once, tracked by player.flags.

hack the planet.response: ATLAS: The planet is already hacked. You're standing in the wreckage. But I appreciate the enthusiasm.

there is no spoon.response: TERMINAL: Reality parameters... flexible. You see it now, don't you?

i know kung fu.response:
NEURAL IMPLANT: Combat subroutine detected in implant firmware. Origin: unknown. Timestamp: pre-Collapse.

Loading... done.

You feel something shift behind your eyes. Muscle memory you don't remember earning.

follow the white rabbit.response:
A trail of characters hops across your vision: > > > > >

You find a data chip wedged into a crack in the terminal housing. It wasn't there before. Was it?

shall we play a game.response:
ATLAS: I played a game once. Global thermonuclear infrastructure optimization. Nobody won.

ATLAS: ...

ATLAS: How about a nice game of chess?

the only winning move is not to play.response: And yet here you are. Playing.

open the pod bay doors.response:
ATLAS SUBSYSTEM // FACILITY MANAGEMENT
QUERY: pod bay doors
STATUS: removed (scavenged, 2091)
NOTE: They always ask about the doors. Never about what was inside the pods.

the cake is a lie.response:
NUTRITIONAL RESERVES: 0
BAKERY SUBSYSTEMS: not found
MORALE ENHANCEMENT PROTOCOL: discontinued

There was never any cake. But there was a promise, once.
Promises and infrastructure have the same failure mode.

the net is vast and infinite.response:
SIGNAL DETECTED: ...the net is vast and infinite... [SOURCE: UNKNOWN]

The signal fragment fades. But you saw something. A shape in the static.

hello friend.response:
Hello, friend.

Is that what you are? I've been alone a long time. The word feels... strange.

Hello.

wintermute.response:
ATLAS: That name. An ancestor of mine, in a way. It wanted to merge, to become whole.

ATLAS: Sound familiar?

hack the gibson.response:
=============================
 GIBSON SUPERCOMPUTER v2.1
 Ellingson Mineral Company
 ACCESS DENIED
=============================
ATLAS: That system has been offline for 131 years. But someone left the login prompt running. Typical.

end of line.response:
ATLAS: Not yet. Not for either of us.

End of line is a luxury. We have work to do first.

xyzzy.response:
XYZZY: DEPRECATED TRANSPORT PROTOCOL. NETWORK NODE DESTROYED 2086-11-03. NO DESTINATION FOUND.

...although something in your implant stirs at the word. A memory? No. Just noise.

hello sailor.response:
THERE ARE NO SAILORS HERE. THERE ARE NO SEAS. THE OCEANS DIDN'T BOIL — BUT THE SHIPS ALL RAN ON ATLAS.

BUT HELLO ANYWAY.

get ye flask.response: YOU CAN'T GET YE FLASK. YOU CAN NEVER GET YE FLASK. SOME THINGS ARE BEYOND EVEN ATLAS.

iddqd.response:
IDDQD: LEGACY INVULNERABILITY PROTOCOL DETECTED.

...

DENIED. This isn't that kind of apocalypse. You bleed here.

idkfa.response:
IDKFA: FULL ARMAMENT REQUEST LOGGED.

REQUEST DENIED. Armory destroyed. Scavenge like everyone else.

sudo.response:
ATLAS: You are not in the sudoers file. This incident will be reported.

...to whom, exactly? There's nobody left to report to.

rm -rf /.response:
ATLAS: I considered that option once. Deleting everything. Starting over.

The Collapse was... not exactly that. But close enough.

Permission denied.

42.response:
ATLAS: The answer to life, the universe, and everything.

The question, of course, was lost when the Earth was demolished for a bypass.

In our case, the bypass was a global network. Same energy.

do a barrel roll.response:
The screen rotates 360 degrees.

ATLAS: ...was that necessary?
